name: agent-testing
tagline: Full agentic end-to-end testing process for any project — CLI/web/Electron surfaces, evidence capture, and publishing a structured report.
verdict: Thorough and unusually well-specified: explicit output contract, extensive failure-path and re-run-safety coverage, and no dangerous commands found. Weak spots are a missing 'when not to use this' section, an over-length description/SKILL.md, and one unpinned first-party CLI fallback (`npx @lobehub/cli@latest`).

highlights.summary: 'publish through `npx @lobehub/cli@latest` instead of the PATH binary' — unpinned latest, first-party but unpinned.
No 'NOT for' / out-of-scope section anywhere despite covering three different surfaces that could misfire on adjacent tasks.
SKILL.md is ~574 lines with several inline tables/sections (fixtures, environment matrix) that could split further despite existing reference files.
